1	// Package bead implements the on-disk bead tracker.
2	//
3	// Size envelope (ddx-f8a11202):
4	//
5	// Individual bead fields — description, acceptance, notes, and each event's
6	// body — are bounded by MaxFieldBytes (65,535 bytes). This matches upstream
7	// bd's Dolt TEXT column limit so DDx-authored beads can always round-trip
8	// through `bd import`. Writers exceeding the cap are truncated with a
9	// `…[truncated, N bytes]` marker at AppendEvent; callers that need to
10	// preserve the full payload (notably reviewer streams) should persist to a
11	// sidecar artifact under `.ddx/executions/<id>/` and record a path
12	// reference in the event body.
13	//
14	// On the read side, scanners use a 16MB buffer — real-world incidents have
15	// produced 1.46MB bead rows and ~7MB session-log rows when writers bypassed
16	// the cap. 16MB comfortably fits a bead with dozens of maxed-out fields and
17	// matches the scanner in the agent package.
18	package bead
19	
20	import (
21		"bufio"
22		"bytes"
23		"context"
24		"crypto/rand"
25		"encoding/hex"
26		"encoding/json"
27		"fmt"
28		"os"
29		"path/filepath"
30		"sort"
31		"strconv"
32		"strings"
33		"time"
34	
35		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
36		gitpkg "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
37	)
38	
39	// Store manages beads via a pluggable backend.
40	type Store struct {
41		Collection string
42		Dir        string
43		File       string
44		Prefix     string
45		LockDir    string
46		LockWait   time.Duration
47		backend    RawBackend // nil means use built-in JSONL
48	}
49	
50	// Compile-time check: *Store satisfies the high-level Backend interface
51	// declared in backend.go. This is the contract chaos_test.go and (eventually)
52	// cli/cmd callers program against.
53	var _ Backend = (*Store)(nil)
54	
55	type StoreOption func(*Store)
56	
57	// WithCollection selects the logical bead collection. The default collection
58	// remains "beads", which maps to "beads.jsonl" in the JSONL backend.
59	func WithCollection(name string) StoreOption {
60		return func(s *Store) {
61			if cleaned := normalizeCollection(name); cleaned != "" {
62				s.Collection = cleaned
63			}
64		}
65	}
66	
67	// NewStore creates a store with the given directory.
68	// Defaults can be overridden via options or environment.
69	func NewStore(dir string, opts ...StoreOption) *Store {
70		if dir == "" {
71			dir = envOr("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
72		}
73		workingDir := dir
74		if filepath.Base(dir) == ".ddx" {
75			workingDir = filepath.Dir(dir)
76		}
77		prefix := envOr("DDX_BEAD_PREFIX", "")
78		var configBackend string
79		if cfg, err := config.LoadWithWorkingDir(workingDir); err == nil && cfg != nil && cfg.Bead != nil {
80			if prefix == "" && cfg.Bead.IDPrefix != "" {
81				prefix = cfg.Bead.IDPrefix
82			}
83			configBackend = cfg.Bead.Backend
84		}
85		if prefix == "" {
86			prefix = detectPrefix(workingDir)
87		}
88		// Backend selection: env var wins, then config, then jsonl default.
89		backendType := os.Getenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND")
90		if backendType == "" {
91			backendType = configBackend
92		}
93		if backendType == "" {
94			backendType = BackendJSONL
95		}
96	
97		s := &Store{
98			Collection: DefaultCollection,
99			Dir:        dir,
100			Prefix:     prefix,

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"
6	)
7	
8	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
9	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
10	// implement it. New backends should implement Backend directly; RawBackend is
11	// retained for the JSONL/external composition path that Store uses internally.
12	// Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops, events,
13	// archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and are
14	// composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
15	type RawBackend interface {
16		Init() error
17		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
18		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
19		WithLock(fn func() error) error
20	}
21	
22	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
23	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
24	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
25	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
26	// JSONL export/import.
27	//
28	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
29	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
30	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
31	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
32	//
33	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
34	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
35	type Backend interface {
36		// Foundational
37		Init(args ...any) error
38		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
39	
40		// CRUD
41		Create(args ...any) error
42		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
43		Update(args ...any) error
44		Close(args ...any) error
45	
46		// Claim
47		Claim(id, assignee string) error
48		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
49		Unclaim(id string) error
50	
51		// Query
52		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
53		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
54		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
55	
56		// Dep ops
57		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
58		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
59		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
60	
61		// Events
62		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
63		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
64	
65		// Archive split
66		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
67		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
68	
69		// JSONL interchange
70		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
71		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
72	}
73	
74	// TD-027 foundation interfaces. These are additive and intentionally do not
75	// change the legacy Store-backed interface above in this bead slice.

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    git-config-health:
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before committing."

    beads-tracker-health:
      run: "sh scripts/beads-tracker-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Beads tracker has unstaged changes. Run 'git add .ddx/beads.jsonl'."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
    go-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
        # under analysistest's GOPATH-style loader.
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '/testdata/' || true)
        [ -z "$packages" ] && exit 0
        for pkg in $packages; do
          golangci-lint run --timeout=60s --new-from-rev=HEAD~1 "$pkg"
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go build - only for changed packages
    go-build:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Build"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: 'go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...'
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
    # unrelated commits. Stages B–E migrate the remaining historical
    # sites; full-tree enforcement lives in the `ci` block below.
    evidence-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Unbounded prompt sink detected in staged file. Route through internal/evidence or add `// evidence:allow-unbounded reason=\"...\"`."

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      env:
        CGO_ENABLED: "1"
        SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS: "true"

    # Secrets scanning - prefer gitleaks when available
    secrets:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Scan for secrets"
      run: |
        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          gitleaks protect --staged --verbose --redact
        else
          # Fallback to staged-added-line pattern matching when gitleaks is absent.
          # Scanning whole tracker JSONL files causes noisy false positives on prose.
          added_lines=$(git diff --cached --unified=0 --no-color -- {staged_files} | grep '^+' | grep -v '^+++')

          patterns='((api[_-]?key|apikey|secret|password|passwd|pwd|credential|private[_-]?key|access[_-]?token|auth[_-]?token)[[:space:]]*[:=][[:space:]]*["'"'"'][A-Za-z0-9_./+=-]{20,})|(Authorization:[[:space:]]*Bearer[[:space:]]+[A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,})'
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E "$patterns"; then
            echo "Potential secrets detected"
            exit 1
          fi

          # AWS keys
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'; then
            echo "AWS key pattern detected"
            exit 1
          fi

          # GitHub tokens
          if printf '%s\n' "$added_lines" | grep -E 'ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}'; then
            echo "GitHub token pattern detected"
            exit 1
          fi
        fi
      exclude:
        - "*.md"
        - "go.sum"
        - "package-lock.json"
        - "*_test.go"
        - "**/dist/**"
        - "scripts/test-secrets-fallback.sh"
      fail_text: "Secrets detected! Remove them or add to .gitleaks.toml if false positive"

    # Binary and large file check
    large-files:
      name: "Check file sizes"
      run: |
        for file in {staged_files}; do
          size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$size" -gt 5242880 ]; then
            echo "Large file detected: $file ($(($size / 1048576))MB)"
            exit 1
          fi
        done
      exclude:
        - "*.png"
        - "*.jpg"
        - "*.jpeg"
        - "*.gif"
        - "*.svg"
        - "*.ico"
        - "*.pdf"
        - "*.woff"
        - "*.woff2"
        - "*.ttf"
        - "*.eot"
        # bead store grows monotonically with queue activity; expected to exceed 5MB
        - ".ddx/beads.jsonl"
        # archive partner accumulates closed beads forever; trends past 5MB
        - ".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl"
      fail_text: "Large files detected. Use Git LFS for files over 5MB"

    # DDx validation - only when relevant files change
    ddx-validate:
      name: "Validate DDx"
      run: |
        # Only run if DDx files are staged
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(\.ddx\.yml|templates/|patterns/|prompts/)'; then
          exit 0
        fi

        # Strip git's local-env-vars before invoking ddx so any git commands
        # the ddx process spawns honor cmd.Dir / -C <dir> rather than the
        # GIT_DIR / GIT_WORK_TREE / etc. inherited from this hook's context.
        # Without this, ddx's git invocations mutate THIS repo (the hook's),
        # producing core.bare=true and stray core.worktree corruption.
        # ddx-64ac553a Phase 0 (defensive triage; real fix is the central
        # git.Command wrapper in Phase 2a).
        unset $(git rev-parse --local-env-vars)

        # Collect staged paths and pass them to --paths so doctor only validates
        # the checks relevant to the staged files (config or resource), skipping
        # global installation checks (e.g. legacy skill symlinks) that are
        # unrelated to the content of the staged change. [ddx-777f4a09]
        staged_paths="$(git diff --cached --name-only | tr '\n' ' ')"

        # Check for DDx command availability
        if command -v ddx >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          ddx doctor --paths "$staged_paths" >/dev/null 2>&1
        elif [ -x "./cli/ddx" ]; then
          ./cli/ddx doctor --paths "$staged_paths" >/dev/null 2>&1
        fi
      fail_text: "DDx validation failed. Run 'ddx doctor' for details"

    skill-schema:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Validate skill metadata"
      run: |
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^(library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md|cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/skills/[^/]+/SKILL\.md)$'; then
          exit 0
        fi
        make skill-schema
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    sync-embedded-default-plugin:
      name: "Sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        if ! git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '^library/(skills/|package\.yaml$)'; then
          exit 0
        fi
        make copy-skills
        cd .. && git add cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library/
      stage_fixed: true
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Failed to sync cli/internal/registry/defaultplugin/library from library/. Run 'cd cli && make copy-skills' manually and stage the result."

    design-md-lint:
      name: "DESIGN.md lint"
      glob: ".stitch/DESIGN.md"
      run: |
        if ! command -v bun >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "bun not found; skipping DESIGN.md lint"
          exit 0
        fi
        bun install --frozen-lockfile 2>/dev/null || bun install
        bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
      fail_text: "DESIGN.md lint failed. Run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md"

    test-engineer-persona-drift:
      name: "Guard test-engineer persona (ddx-482fad2f)"
      glob: "library/personas/test-engineer.md"
      run: |
        if grep -q "Mock all external dependencies" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
          echo "ERROR: 'Mock all external dependencies' is forbidden in the test-engineer persona (see ddx-482fad2f)"
          exit 1
        fi
        if grep -q "test doubles (mocks" library/personas/test-engineer.md; then
          echo "ERROR: 'test doubles (mocks, stubs, spies)' lists mocks and stubs as equivalent — forbidden (see ddx-482fad2f)"
          exit 1
        fi
      fail_text: "test-engineer persona contains forbidden mock-promotion phrases"

    no-tmp-in-test-factory:
      name: "Guard NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") in tests (ddx-9063101c)"
      glob: "cli/cmd/*_test.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/cmd/[^/]+_test\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        bad=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs grep -lF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")' 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$bad" ]; then
          printf '%s\n' "$bad" | xargs grep -nF 'NewCommandFactory("/tmp")'
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "NewCommandFactory(\"/tmp\") found in cli/cmd/*_test.go — use t.TempDir() instead"

    ddxroot-path-audit:
      name: "Guard hardcoded DDx state-root callsites"
      run: |
        if rg -n 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"|filepath\.Join\(\s*(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*RelPath\s*\)|(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*\+\s*"/\.ddx(?:/|")' cli/ --type go | grep -Ev '(^cli/internal/ddxroot/)|(_test\.go$)'; then
          echo "Hardcoded DDx state-root callsites found outside cli/internal/ddxroot/ and approved test fixtures"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Hardcoded DDx state-root callsites found outside cli/internal/ddxroot/. Route project state paths through internal/ddxroot."

    skill-dir-anchor:
      name: "Skill dirs must have SKILL.md"
      run: |
        failed=0
        for base in .agents/skills .claude/skills; do
          [ -d "$base" ] || continue
          for d in $(ls -A "$base"); do
            full="$base/$d"
            [ -d "$full" ] || continue
            if [ ! -f "$full/SKILL.md" ]; then
              echo "ERROR: skill-dir $full has no SKILL.md; skill directories must be real skills with a SKILL.md anchor. If this is contamination, remove the directory. If this is a new skill, add a SKILL.md."
              failed=1
            fi
          done
        done
        exit $failed
      fail_text: "Skill directory missing SKILL.md. Either add SKILL.md (new skill) or remove the directory (contamination)."

# ====================
# CI/CD - Full validation (all files)
# ====================
ci:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Full conflict check
    conflicts-all:
      name: "Check all merge conflicts"
      run: |
        # Check for actual merge conflict markers (exactly 7 characters followed by space or newline)
        conflicts=$(find . -type f ! -path "./.git/*" ! -path "./cli/.git/*" ! -name "lefthook.yml" \
          ! -path "*/node_modules/*" ! -path "*/.svelte-kit/*" ! -path "./cli/internal/server/frontend/build/*" \
          ! -path "./website/public/*" ! -path "./cli/build/*" \
          -exec grep -l "^<<<<<<<[ \t]\|^=======\$\|^>>>>>>>[ \t]\|^||||||[ \t]" {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflicts" ]; then
          echo "Merge conflict markers found in: $conflicts"
          exit 1
        fi

    # Full secrets scan
    secrets-all:
      <<: *security_checks
      name: "Full secrets scan"
      run: |
        if command -v gitleaks >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          gitleaks detect --verbose --redact
        else
          echo "Consider installing gitleaks for comprehensive secret scanning"
        fi

    # Go full test suite
    go-test-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Full Go test suite"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go test -race -cover ./...'

    bead-schema:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Bead Schema Guard"
      run: 'make bead-schema'

    skill-schema:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Skill Metadata Guard"
      run: 'make skill-schema'

    # Go formatting check all files
    go-fmt-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Check all Go formatting"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Unformatted files: $unformatted"
          exit 1
        fi

    # Go lint all files
    go-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Lint all Go files"
      root: "cli/"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        golangci-lint run --timeout=5m

    # Go build all targets
    go-build-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Build all targets"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go build -v ./...'

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage. Repo-wide
    # invariant check; runs in CI even when no Go files were staged
    # in the triggering commit so historical regressions still surface.
    evidence-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Evidence Lint (no-unbounded-prompts) — full tree"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint ./...'

    # SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: full-tree gate. The pre-commit hook
    # only checks staged-package diagnostics; this CI gate enforces the
    # post-cleanup invariant — zero forbidden runtime fields and zero
    # legacy *Options types anywhere in cli/. Activated now that the
    # RunOptions/CompareOptions/QuorumOptions/ExecuteBeadOptions
    # retirement beads have all landed (ddx-ade5ebb3, ddx-a8eaef0a).
    runtime-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options) — full tree"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint ./...'

    ddxroot-path-audit-all:
      name: "Guard hardcoded DDx state-root callsites — full tree"
      run: |
        if rg -n 'filepath\.Join\([^)]*"\.ddx"|filepath\.Join\(\s*(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*,\s*[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*RelPath\s*\)|(projectRoot|[A-Za-z0-9_\.]*WorkingDir)\s*\+\s*"/\.ddx(?:/|")' cli/ --type go | grep -Ev '(^cli/internal/ddxroot/)|(_test\.go$)'; then
          echo "Hardcoded DDx state-root callsites found outside cli/internal/ddxroot/ and approved test fixtures"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0

    # FEAT-006 routing-lint: dead-code / anti-reintroduction gate for
    # the compensating DDx-side routing helpers retired by ddx-3bd7396a
    # (parent epic ddx-fdd3ea36 §AC#7, cleanup bead ddx-653f6ac9). Fails
    # CI if any of ResolveProfileLadder, ResolveTierModelRef,
    # AdaptiveMinTier, workersByHarness, or the retired --escalate /
    # --override-model / agent.routing.profile_ladders /
    # agent.routing.model_overrides flag/config tokens reappear in
    # cli/ Go source.
    routing-lint-all:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Routing Lint (FEAT-006 compensating-routing helpers retired) — full tree"
      root: "cli/"
      run: 'go run ./tools/lint/routinglint/cmd/routinglint ./...'

    docs-routing-lint:
      name: "Docs Routing Lint (legacy DDx routing surfaces allowlisted)"
      run: 'bash scripts/check-legacy-routing-docs.sh'

    # YAML validation
    yaml-validate:
      name: "Validate YAML files"
      glob: "**/*.{yml,yaml}"
      run: |
        if command -v yq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          for file in {all_files}; do
            yq eval . "$file" >/dev/null || exit 1
          done
        fi

# ====================
# Pre-push hooks - Extended validation
# ====================
pre-push:
  parallel: false

  commands:
    git-config-health:
      name: "Git config health"
      run: "sh scripts/git-config-health.sh"
      fail_text: "Local Git config is invalid. Repair core.bare/core.worktree/core.hooksPath and fixture identity before pushing."
      tags: [policy]

    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
    merge-policy:
      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
      run: |
        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
        remote="${1:-origin}"
        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
          exit 0
        fi
        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
          echo ""
          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      tags: [policy]

    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
    no-debug:
      name: "No debug statements"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
          status=$?
          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
            exit 0
          fi
          exit 1
        fi

# Skip configuration
# Usage: LEFTHOOK_EXCLUDE=go-test,secrets git commit
# Or: LEFTHOOK=0 git commit (skip all hooks)

# Install hooks: lefthook install
# Run manually: lefthook run pre-commit
# Run CI checks: lefthook run ci
